description: Create a GitLab CI pipeline using node:17-alpine as the default Docker image and a global before_script that echoes CPU and memory info, prints node and npm versions, shows the working directory and lists top‚Äëlevel directories. In the install stage add an install job that caches ${CI_COMMIT_REF_SLUG} node_modules, package-lock.json and the digest, demo/redux and demo/simple package directories (policy push) and runs the exact command `npm run bootstrap`. In the build stage add a build:connect job (pull cache of node_modules) that executes `PARALLEL='off' npm run lerna -- run build --scope ui-router-react-connect --include-filtered-dependencies` and stores `packages/connect/dist` as a 2‚Äëhour artifact, and a build:digest job (same cache) that runs `PARALLEL='off' npm run lerna -- run build --scope ui-router-react-digest --include-filtered-dependencies`, then `mkdir public` and `npm run copy:stats`, publishing both `packages/digest/dist` and the `public` folder as artifacts. In the test stage define lint (pull node_modules cache, depends on the two build jobs, runs `npm run lint`), unit (same cache, depends on the builds, runs `npm run unit:ci`, `npm run unit:merge`, `npm run copy:stats` and produces a JUnit report at `reports/junit.xml` plus the `public` folder), test_demo (pull cache, depends on the builds, runs `npm run copy:stats` and archives the `public` folder), build:demo-simple and build:demo-redux (both pull cache, depend on the builds, run their respective lerna build commands with `PARALLEL='off' npm run lerna -- run build --scope ui-router-react-digest-simple-demo` or `...-redux-demo` followed by `npm run copy:stats`, and publish the `public` folder as a 2‚Äëhour artifact). In the e2e stage add chrome and firefox jobs that use the image `registry.hub.docker.com/atlassianlabs/docker-node-jdk-chrome-firefox:latest`, pull the node_modules cache, depend on lint, unit and the build jobs, execute `ln -sf /opt/google/chrome/google-chrome /usr/bin/google-chrome && google-chrome --version && npm run e2e:chrome` (or the analogous Firefox commands) and always upload `public/` and `reports/screenshots/` as artifacts. Finally, in the deploy stage create a pages job (image node:17) and a release job (image node:17) that both run only on the `master` branch of `wallzero/ui-router-react-digest`, exclude tags, use the same pull cache, depend on build:digest, build:connect and chrome, where pages runs `echo "deploying demo to gitlab pages"` and publishes the `public/` directory as a GitLab Pages artifact, and release runs `npm run semantic-release` and `npm run release`.
